# State: GENERATE

GENERATE builds kb.html from KB content using the resolved section manifest produced by
PROFILE; it is selected after PROFILE completes or when a stored manifest already exists
and the KB is stale.

---

### 1. Load minimum grade

```
if --grade present: MIN_GRADE = flag value (and update .aid/settings.yml summary.minimum_grade via /aid-config)
else: MIN_GRADE = bash .github/aid/scripts/config/read-setting.sh --skill summary --key minimum_grade --default A
```

Persist `--grade` overrides to `.aid/settings.yml` (via `/aid-config` or direct YAML edit). Do NOT write minimum_grade to STATE.md — STATE.md is for run-state (Q&A, Review History, Summarization History), not config.

---

### 2. Read all resolved KB documents

For each resolved doc from the PROFILE manifest, read `.aid/knowledge/{doc}.md` and extract:

- **Section heading / title** — derive from `objective:` (noun-phrase) as a newcomer-phrased
  title; fallback to doc filename de-slugified (e.g. `module-map` → "Module Map").
- **One-line description** — `summary:` (one-sentence scope); fallback `objective:` →
  `intent:` → first paragraph after H1.
- **Purpose blurb** — `objective:` (purpose noun-phrase); fallback `intent:` → first
  paragraph. Feeds the newcomer "why this matters" framing.
- **Tier / prominence** — `kb-category:` (`primary` | `extension` | `meta`); absent →
  treat as `primary`.
- **Keyword pills** — `tags:` list; omit if absent (no placeholder).
- **See-also cross-links** — `see_also:` list; omit if absent.
- **Key facts** — numbers, names, version pins from the doc body (T1+T2 tier facts; skip
  T3 line-counts — those are tracked separately in metrics.md).
- **Generated-doc flag** — if frontmatter `source: generated`, render the section with a
  small "(auto-generated by `{generator}`)" attribution line (mark generated files).

**Do NOT render `audience:` as a role-badge.** The KB's dual-audience / agent-frontmatter
framing does not apply to the summary. The newcomer does not care which agent-role a doc
targets; audience leaks the KB's machine-oriented framing into a newcomer-facing product.

The KB is **authoritative**. Do not re-grade it, re-validate it, or contradict it.
If the KB says X, the HTML says X.

---

### 3. Derive the section manifest from the resolved doc-set

Using the ordered section manifest from PROFILE:

**Section structure per doc:**
- One section per resolved doc (no more, no fewer — completeness = coverage of the
  resolved doc-set, never a fixed section list or diagram count).
- Section attributes: heading, one-line description, purpose blurb, keyword pills,
  see-also links, source citation (relative `./doc.md` link), and tier-keyed prominence.

**Bespoke component assignment (by filename — deterministic, no fallback for these three):**

The component-selection algorithm is applied in this order:

1. If the resolved doc filename is `domain-glossary.md` →
   **Glossary / definition component** (`id="glossary"` on the section; per-term
   `id="gloss-{slug}"` anchors). Render the Concept Spine terms as `.gloss-card` items
   inside a `.gloss-grid`. Draw from the "Definition-as-used-here" / "Relates-to" /
   "Aliases" fields in each `## {Term}` subsection of the doc body.
   HTML template: `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/section-templates/bespoke-components.md §1`.
   **Rendered as content, not linked.** A newcomer reads the definitions in the page;
   the only link to `domain-glossary.md` is the footer "Source:" citation.

2. If the resolved doc filename is `decisions.md` →
   **Decision / ADR card component** (`id="decisions"` on the section; per-ADR
   `id="adr-d{N}"` anchors). Render each `## D{N} — {Title}` entry as an `.adr-card`
   inside an `.adr-list`. Draw from the Decision / Alternatives rejected / Constraint
   fields in each entry.
   HTML template: `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/section-templates/bespoke-components.md §2`.
   **Rendered as content, not linked.** A newcomer reads the ADR rationale in the page;
   the only link to `decisions.md` is the footer "Source:" citation.

3. If the resolved doc filename is `capability-inventory.md` →
   **Capability entry component** (`id="capabilities"` on the section; per-capability
   `id="cap-{slug}"` anchors). Render each capability row as a `.cap-card` inside a
   `.cap-grid`, grouped by the source doc's section structure (pipeline / on-demand).
   HTML template: `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/section-templates/bespoke-components.md §3`.
   **Rendered as content, not linked.** A newcomer reads what the project can do in the
   page; the only link to `capability-inventory.md` is the footer "Source:" citation.

Component styles are in
`.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/component-css.css` (the three
"Concept-first content components" sections: `.gloss-*`, `.adr-*`, `.cap-*`).
These classes use the existing design tokens — do not restyle or override the page theme.

**Generic fallback (all other resolved docs — no doc is ever skipped):**
- **`kb-category: primary`** → full section (featured-ness for domain-salient docs).
  Choose the best format per fact: tables for catalogs, cards for grids, prose for
  narrative, infographic for structure. A doc with `kb-category: primary` MAY carry an
  infographic if the content warrants it.
- **`kb-category: extension`** → supporting / lower-prominence section. Same per-fact
  format selection as primary but rendered as a secondary section.
- **`kb-category: meta`** (`external-sources.md`, `README.md`) → compact prose /
  reference list. Orientation, not a newcomer-concern section. May be folded into the
  KB Index.

**No resolved doc is ever skipped.** "No bespoke component" is never "no section" — the
generic fallback guarantees coverage. Rendering hints by domain / tier are in
`.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/section-templates/auto-detect.md` (tier hints)
and the domain-specific `section-templates/{domain}.md` files (per-doc hints).

**Stable anchor ID contract:** the three bespoke component sections use fixed anchor IDs
(`glossary`, `decisions`, `capabilities`) so the "At a Glance" teaser links (§4) and the
TOC can reliably point to them. Per-item anchors (`gloss-{slug}`, `adr-d{N}`,
`cap-{slug}`) use the same slugging rule: term/title lowercased, spaces replaced by
hyphens, non-alphanumeric characters dropped.

---

### 4. Build the "At a Glance" section (always first)

"At a Glance" is synthesized from:

| Input | Source |
|-------|--------|
| **What this is** (lead) | project name (STATE.md / build file) + domain framing (I2) + `domain-glossary.md` / `capability-inventory.md` `objective:` |
| **What it does** | `capability-inventory.md` `objective:`/`summary:` + top capability rows |
| **Key vocabulary teaser** | first few `domain-glossary.md` terms (a taste of the project's language, linking down to the Glossary section) |
| **Key decisions teaser** | first `decisions.md` ADR title(s) ("the big calls", linking down to the ADR cards section) |

Write "At a Glance" as a **newcomer-friendly plain-language section**: "This is a {domain
framing} that {what it does}." Counts (module count, test count, etc.) may appear as a
small secondary detail but do **not** lead and are not the section's purpose. The lead
is *what & why*, not LOC or metric cards.

If `domain-glossary.md` or `capability-inventory.md` are absent from the resolved doc-set,
omit their respective teaser; the "At a Glance" section still renders with the available
inputs.

---

### 5. Data-driven authoring into the multi-source layout

> **LLM role (narrowed by Change 6 / FR-50):** The LLM authors the **per-component content**
> of each section file (the prose, definitions, ADR cards, capability entries, tables, and
> infographics that fill the inner content area of each section). The LLM does NOT hand-write
> the page HTML shell, section ordering, or assembly logic — those are mechanical and handled
> by the assembler from the manifest. Judgment is exercised only where irreducible: newcomer-
> friendly prose, choosing best-format-per-fact, and the tone/clarity of content within each
> component. Assembly, section ordering, inlining, and shell structure are deterministic.

**Authoring layout (`.aid/knowledge/summary-src/`)** — distribution is still a single
self-contained HTML file, but sources are split for maintainability:

```
.aid/knowledge/summary-src/
├── skeleton-head.html          # <!DOCTYPE> to opening <main> (includes inlined CSS)
├── sections/
│   ├── 00-at-a-glance.html
│   ├── 01-{resolved-doc-slug}.html   # one file per resolved doc (in manifest order)
│   ├── 02-{resolved-doc-slug}.html
│   └── ...
├── section-manifest.txt        # DETERMINISM FILE: ordered section filenames (one per line)
├── skeleton-foot.html          # </main> to closing shell elements
└── post-script.html            # lightbox.js + closing </body>
```

**`section-manifest.txt` — the determinism contract (FR-50):**
Write this file BEFORE authoring sections. It is the machine-readable ordered list of
section filenames that drives deterministic assembly (same manifest = same section order =
same structural output). Format: one filename per line (relative to `sections/`), blank
lines and `#` comment lines ignored:

```
# section-manifest.txt -- generated by GENERATE; do not edit by hand.
# Ordered from: discovery.doc_set + frontmatter + section ordering rule (state-profile.md).
# Same manifest -> same structural output (reproducible + auditable, FR-50).
00-at-a-glance.html
01-domain-glossary.html
02-decisions.html
03-capability-inventory.html
04-architecture.html
05-process-architecture.html
# ... one line per resolved doc (in manifest order) ...
NN-kb-index.html
```

The ordering rule (from `state-profile.md` Step 4) is:
1. `00-at-a-glance.html` (always first — synthesized, not a doc section)
2. Concept-first trio (if present in resolved doc-set): glossary, decisions, capabilities
3. Remaining `kb-category: primary` docs in `discovery.doc_set` list order
4. `kb-category: extension` docs in `discovery.doc_set` list order
5. `kb-category: meta` docs last
6. `NN-kb-index.html` (always last — full resolved-doc table)

Number the filenames sequentially (`00-`, `01-`, etc.) matching the manifest order so the
filesystem listing is also ordered; the assembler reads the manifest, not the filesystem
glob, for authoritative ordering.

Use `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/html-skeleton.html` as the seed when
scaffolding `skeleton-head.html` for the first time. The outer page shell (top bar, side
panel, search, nav chrome) is kept consistent with `home.html` and the CLI `index.html`.
Only the inner content area (the sections) is domain-specific.

The skeleton already includes the full export chrome in the `.controls` block:
- **"Export as Markdown"** button (`#btn-export-md`) — downloads the embedded KB payload
  as `knowledge-base-export.md`; disabled gracefully when the payload element is absent.
- **"Export as PDF"** button (`#btn-export-pdf`) — opens all `<details>` (including
  `details.accord`) before calling `window.print()` so closed accordions are captured;
  restores prior open states via the `afterprint` event.

Both handlers are in `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/lightbox.js` (inlined by
`post-script.html`) and are wired into the existing `DOMContentLoaded` bootstrap via
`initExportMarkdown()` and `initExportPDF()`. No additional wiring is required when
scaffolding from the seed. Print CSS (`@media print`) in
`.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/component-css.css` hides nav/controls/lightbox,
forces light-theme rendering when the page is in dark mode, and inserts page breaks between
top-level sections.

Inject into the seed when scaffolding `skeleton-head.html`:
- Hero with project name (read from `.aid/knowledge/STATE.md` or `pom.xml` / `package.json`).
- Inlined CSS from `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/component-css.css`.
- **noscript doc list** derived from the resolved doc-set (see §6 below) — not a hardcoded
  list.

Author each section file under `sections/` from KB content:
- Section structure follows the manifest (§3 above): bespoke component for the three
  well-known docs; generic fallback for all others.
- Per-section content drawn from KB (cite source via relative `./xxx.md` link).
- Rendering hints are in `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/section-templates/`
  (keyed by `kb-category`/spine-dimension — NOT by project-type profile).
- **LLM scope per section file:** author the inner content HTML only (the `<section>` body).
  Do not write the outer shell (top bar, side panel, nav, footer) — those come from the
  skeleton templates and are assembled mechanically.

`post-script.html` inlines `.github/aid/templates/knowledge-summary/lightbox.js`.

**Parallel-safe authoring** — different agents/passes can edit different `sections/NN.html`
files concurrently without merge conflicts, since each section is its own file.

---

### 6. Derive the noscript doc list

The `noscript` region in the skeleton must list the resolved docs so a user with JS off
can still navigate to the source markdown files. Derive the list at generation time:

```html
<noscript>
  <div class="noscript-fallback">
    <h2>JavaScript required</h2>
    <p>This summary uses JavaScript to power the theme toggle, lightbox, and scrollspy.
       With JavaScript disabled, the interactive features will not work.</p>
    <p>Either enable JavaScript, or read the source markdown files directly in
       <code>.aid/knowledge/</code>:</p>
    <ul>
      <li><a href="./INDEX.md">INDEX.md</a> -- Knowledge Base map</li>
      <!-- One <li> per resolved doc-set entry (in manifest order): -->
      <li><a href="./{doc}.md">{doc}.md</a> -- {summary: value or doc name de-slugified}</li>
      ...
    </ul>
  </div>
</noscript>
```

Always lead with `INDEX.md`. Then one `<li>` per resolved doc, in manifest order. The
one-line text comes from each doc's `summary:` frontmatter; fallback to the filename
de-slugified. **No hardcoded doc list survives in the skeleton or any template.**

---

### 7. Build the Markdown export payload

Before assembling `kb.html`, generate the hidden Markdown export payload from the KB
source documents. The payload is embedded in `kb.html` as a `<script type="text/markdown">`
block that client-side export buttons read to download a portable `.md` file.

```bash
bash .github/aid/scripts/summarize/build-md-export.sh \
    --kb-dir .aid/knowledge \
    --manifest .aid/knowledge/summary-src/section-manifest.txt \
    --output .aid/knowledge/summary-src/md-export-payload.html
#   reads:  .aid/knowledge/*.md (in manifest section order, frontmatter stripped)
#   writes: .aid/knowledge/summary-src/md-export-payload.html
```

**Payload element contract** (consumed by the client-side export chrome):

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Element** | `<script type="text/markdown" id="kb-md-export" data-encoding="base64">` |
| **Content** | Combined KB source docs, frontmatter stripped, local images embedded as `data:` URIs (`image/svg+xml` for `.svg`; `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/gif`, `image/webp` for raster). The entire payload is **base64-encoded** (UTF-8 bytes → base64 ASCII). The element body is pure base64 alphabet — no escaping needed, lossless round-trip. Remote `http(s)://` image refs are left untouched. |
| **Access** | `const b64 = document.getElementById('kb-md-export').textContent;` (returns the base64 string exactly — no leading/trailing whitespace or newlines) |
| **Decode (UTF-8)** | `new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(b64), c => c.charCodeAt(0)))` — or equivalently `decodeURIComponent(escape(atob(b64)))` — to recover the original Markdown text including any non-ASCII characters |
| **Filename hint** | `'knowledge-base-export.md'` |
| **Location in `kb.html`** | Injected between `skeleton-foot.html` and `post-script.html` by `assemble.sh` when `md-export-payload.html` is present in the source layout dir |

> **PS-mirror note:** The payload injection lives in `assemble.sh` (the bash-only kb.html
> assembler). `assemble-3part.ps1` is a separate byte-concatenation utility that joins
> pre-assembled Part1 + Part2 files; it is not part of the documented GENERATE flow and
> does not require a parallel update for this payload.

Run `build-md-export.sh` **before** `assemble.sh`. The assembler picks up the payload
automatically; if the file is absent it is silently skipped (no error), preserving
backward compatibility for runs that do not need the Markdown export.

---

### 8. Assemble the single-file distribution (deterministic)

Run `.github/aid/scripts/summarize/assemble.sh` with `--manifest` to concatenate the
multi-source layout into the final single-file `kb.html`. The `--manifest` flag is
**required for deterministic assembly** (same manifest = same section order = same
structural output, reproducible + auditable, FR-50):

```bash
mkdir -p .aid/dashboard
bash .github/aid/scripts/summarize/assemble.sh \
    --manifest .aid/knowledge/summary-src/section-manifest.txt \
    --output .aid/dashboard/kb.html
#   reads:  .aid/knowledge/summary-src/{skeleton-head.html,
#             sections/* in manifest order, skeleton-foot.html,
#             md-export-payload.html (if present), post-script.html}
#   writes: .aid/dashboard/kb.html
```

Flags:
- `--src DIR`       — override source layout path (default `.aid/knowledge/summary-src`)
- `--manifest FILE` — section manifest (one filename per line, relative to `sections/`);
                      required for doc-set-deterministic ordering
- `--output PATH`   — override output path (default `.aid/dashboard/kb.html`)

The script validates that all required source files exist and are non-empty before assembling.
Without `--manifest` it falls back to lexical glob order (backward-compatible but not
doc-set-deterministic; always supply `--manifest` for new runs).

The assembled `kb.html` is a **single self-contained file**: all CSS and JS inlined, no
CDN, no split assets, no external framework fetch, **no runtime diagram engine**. Visuals
are authored as inline SVG / HTML+CSS and are pre-rendered at build time (Change 7 / FR-51).
The dashboard server allowlists only `home.html`/`kb.html` — any sub-resource reference
would 404.

---

### 9. Write Knowledge Summary Status

Write initial fields to `.aid/knowledge/STATE.md` `## Knowledge Summary Status`:
```markdown
**Doc-Set Source:** .aid/settings.yml discovery.doc_set
**Doc-Set Count:** {N resolved} of {M total}
**Domain:** {I2 value}
**Domain Source:** .aid/knowledge/STATE.md ## Discovery Domain
**Theme:** default
**Minimum Grade:** {grade}
**Minimum Grade Source:** {.aid/settings.yml summary.minimum_grade | .aid/settings.yml review.minimum_grade | --grade flag | default}
**Machine Grade:** Pending
**Machine Grade Source:** `grade.sh` AUTO_POOL (68 pts)
**Human Grade:** Pending (run `manual-checklist.sh` before APPROVAL)
**Human Grade Source:** `manual-checklist.sh` MANUAL_POOL (K1+K2+V1, 30 pts)
**Overall Grade:** Pending (= min of Machine and Human letter grades)
**User Approved:** no
**Last Run:** {iso8601}
**Trigger Reason:** {initial | stale-after-review-N | --reset | re-approval-only}
**Output:** .aid/dashboard/kb.html
**Output Size:** {MB}
**Last Reviewed KB Date:** {YYYY-MM-DD}
**Last Summary Date:** {YYYY-MM-DD or N/A}
**Writeback Status:** pending
```

Print: `[State: GENERATE] complete.`

**Advance:** **CHAIN** → [State: VALIDATE] (continue inline).
